summary: Legal relationship management software for in-house counsel.
editor_note: The useful bet is that in-house legal teams need relationship memory, not another contract inbox. Sandstone shows intake, matter tracking, document review, legal research, and work across more than 100 integrated tools. Unlike e-billing or CLM systems, it centers the counsel-client relationship and its history. The risk is whether legal teams keep enough clean context in one place for the AI layer to stay useful.

product_evidence.claim: The official site positions Sandstone as legal relationship management software built for in-house counsel and internal business clients.
The product surface shows legal intake, matter tracking, document review, legal research, communication, and task execution inside one workflow.
Sandstone says the system works with more than 100 integrated tools, including Microsoft 365, Google Workspace, Slack, CLM, and e-billing systems.
The funding article says Sandstone raised a $30M Series A led by Lightspeed to bring AI to in-house legal teams.

market_context.primary_user: In-house legal teams managing business requests, matters, documents, and legal knowledge across multiple internal tools.
market_context.differentiation: Sandstone frames legal operations around counsel-client relationship context rather than isolated contracts, tickets, or billing workflows.
market_context.why_now: Corporate legal teams are under pressure to answer more requests with fewer lawyers, while AI can turn matter history and documents into reusable context.

risks.claim: The open question is whether customers can keep legal context current enough across many systems for AI work products to remain reliable.
risks.basis: VentureDex editorial assessment based on official product evidence and funding-source review.
